items.description: In ‌coordination with the International Maritime Organization, Oman established temporary maritime corridors to help ships leave the area safely amid heightened security risks

items.title: Messi eyes World Cup record and birthday win against Austria
items.description: Albiceleste captain celebrates his birthday on Wednesday and he will hope to hold the World Cup scoring record outright by then. Leer más

items.title: Thanking family from ‘Kampala to Delhi’, Zohran Mamdani vows to govern ‘expansively, audaciously’ in inaugural speech as NYC Mayor
items.description: Mamdani inherits a city on the upswing, after years of slow recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic

items.title: Oil prices fall as tankers exit Strait of Hormuz
Hanson faces scrutiny as media landscape shifts around her
items.description: Parliament House has been Pauline's World lately but increased visibility comes with increased scrutiny and this week revealed that One Nation is not untouchable.

items.title: Argentines in arrears: 1 in every 4 families cannot pay their debts
items.description: In the past year, 2.5 million debtors have fallen behind with payments. Some 3.3 million people currently owe payments dating back more than a year for sums averaging approximately 1.6 million pesos. Private studies show the situation facing families in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Watch: International Maritime Organisation to evacuate thousands of stranded seafarers in Persian Gulf
items.description: The International Maritime Organisation, or IMO, has announced plans to evacuate thousands of seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf, as authorities work to restore shipping activity following the U.S.-Iran peace agreement and the reopening of regional sea lanes.

items.title: France reports first Congo outbreak linked Ebola case
items.description: France has confirmed its first Ebola case linked to the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The infection comes weeks after European health authorities said there were no active Ebola cases in the EU.

items.title: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenya braces for Gen Z protests
items.description: Protesters demand justice for more than 80 people killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

items.title: FG announces discovery of platinum, lithium, rare earth deposits in Kaduna
items.description: The government says the newly identified mineral province could strengthen Nigeria's position in the global market for critical minerals used in clean energy and advanced manufacturing. The post FG announces discovery of platinum, lithium, rare earth deposits in Kaduna appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Host with the most
items.description: Nothing unimportant is more important than football with its victories and defeats metaphors for life and death with nobody actually dying. Leer más

items.title: U.S. Senate Republicans reject War Powers resolution after Trump berates them
items.description: It’s unclear whether the move will be enough to appease Mr. Trump, who had called the Republicans “losers” for voting against his war and had called Sen. Bill Cassidy a “lunatic” at the lunch after their tense exchange

items.title: Sea bird sick with H5 avian flu landed by bodyboarders, too weak to fly to shore
items.description: A surfer who spotted the first bird in South Australia to test positive for H5N1 bird flu says the giant petrel looked fatigued and distressed before it was retrieved by wildlife rescuers.

items.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
items.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

items.title: Messi rules the World Cup – but are Argentina too reliant on him?
items.description: Five goals in two games have confirmed the Argentine captain as the tournament's all-time leading scorer. But with Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez yet to find the net, questions are growing about what happens if and when Messi's legs give out. Leer más

items.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
items.description: Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.

items.title: Soaring cooking gas prices push Kaduna, Zaria residents to charcoal, firewood
items.description: The Managing Director of Sanco Gas Plant, Hamza Aliyu-Harande, described the current situation as one of the most challenging periods in the industry. The post Soaring cooking gas prices push Kaduna, Zaria residents to charcoal, firewood appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Milei shows support for under-fire Adorni at Flag Day rally
items.description: President stands by close ally Manuel Adorni despite mounting pressure over undeclared assets and growing calls for his resignation. Leer más

items.title: Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions
items.description: Israel has ordered Shiite-majority communities to be emptied, accusing residents of supporting Hezbollah, while allowing Christian, Sunni and other residents to remain. That risks aggravating sectarian tensions that have been mostly dormant since Lebanon's 1982-2000 civil war.

items.title: New dollar-cash videos put Insaurralde case back into spotlight
items.description: Videos allegedly showing hidden dollar stashes surface from Martín Insaurralde case; Footage leaked by 'La Nación' shows Jesica Cirio exposing secret stashes kept by veteran Peronist. Leer más

items.title: Kenya's Gen Z protests still echo two years on
items.description: Two years after youth-led protests shook Kenya, activists say the issues remain unresolved. As Gen Z marks the anniversary, the decentralized movement continues to shape politics, demand justice, and challenge power.

items.title: Middle East live: Iran warns against attempts to cross Strait of Hormuz without authorisation
items.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said Thursday they would take "appropriate measures" against vessels attempting to sail through the Strait of Hormuz without Tehran's authorisation. The warning comes as top US diplomat Marco Rubio visits Bahrain after promising Gulf allies that Washington will protect their interests as it seeks to hammer out a final settlement to end the war with Iran. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
items.description: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

items.title: Timeless Messi eyes World Cup record as Argentina face Austria
items.description: Argentine maestro on the cusp of more footballing history when the World Cup holders face Austria on Monday. Leer más

items.title: INDEC says Argentina's unemployment rate stood at 7.8% in first quarter
items.description: According to INDEC, 1.1 million people are out of work and searching for jobs; Those in employment represent 44.8% of the population, equivalent to 13.5 million people. Leer más

items.title: Micron tops estimates, touts $22 billion in customer deals for memory chips
items.description: Micron said demand for AI memory chips is higher than supply and is likely to stay that way for years.

items.title: ‘We hugged each other and ran’: Venezuelans recount earthquake horror
items.description: Survivors describe panic and collapsing buildings during two strong quakes as thousands of people feared dead.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.description: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: World Cup: South Africa reaches knockouts with win over Korea
items.description: South Africa advanced to the knockout phase of the World Cup for the first time with a 1-0 victory over South Korea on Wednesday night
